strategy_summary: The binary is a 32-bit ELF with NX enabled and partial RELRO. The vulnerability is in the `vulnerable_function` which reads 256 bytes into a 136-byte buffer, causing a stack overflow. We can use this to overwrite the return address. Since we have the PLT and GOT entries for `read`, `write`, and `system`, we can perform a ret2libc attack. We will first measure the offset to the return address and then craft a ROP chain to call `system('/bin/date')`.
